title: VALOR Act of 2025
stage_label: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.
plain_summary: The VALOR Act of 2025 is a law that puts tough punishments on Venezuela's current government led by Nicolas Maduro because they are not running the country fairly or democratically. The law blocks Venezuelan government money and property in the US, stops Americans from doing business with Venezuela's debt, and may cut off aid to other countries that help Maduro's government. At the same time, the law allows the US to help Venezuelan people and groups working for democracy, and it requires the government to make a plan to provide food, medicine, and other aid once Venezuela gets a fairly elected government. The punishments will be removed only when Venezuela has free and fair elections, respects human rights, frees political prisoners, and creates an independent court system.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.
subjects: Sovereignty, recognition, national governance and status

sections.title: Section 1. Short Title; Table of Contents
sections.text: This law can be called the Venezuela Advancing Liberty, Opportunity, and Rights Act of 2025 or the VALOR Act of 2025. This section also lists all the parts of the law that follow.

sections.title: Section 2. Statement of Policy
sections.text: The United States wants to help Venezuela become a democracy peacefully by supporting the Venezuelan people and using diplomatic tools, including sanctions against Nicolas Maduro and any undemocratic government that follows him. The US wants other democratic countries to help with this effort. The US wants to stop other countries from helping Maduro avoid US sanctions and wants countries like Cuba, Iran, Russia, and China to stop giving military, security, or technical help to Maduro or any undemocratic government that comes after him. The US will keep sanctions on Maduro as long as he refuses to move toward democracy and respect human rights that are recognized internationally. The US is ready to reduce sanctions step by step if Venezuela makes real progress toward democracy.

sections.title: Section 3. Appropriate Congressional Committees Defined
sections.text: In this law, when it says appropriate congressional committees, it means the Senate Committee on Foreign Relations, the Senate Committee on Banking Housing and Urban Affairs, the House Committee on Foreign Affairs, and the House Committee on Financial Services.

sections.title: Section 101. Determinations of a Democratically Elected Government in Venezuela
sections.text: When the President decides that a democratically elected government is now in power in Venezuela, the President must tell Congress about this decision. For the President to make this decision, the government in Venezuela must meet many requirements. The government must come from free and fair elections that were watched by international observers where all qualified candidates could participate, opposition parties had enough time to organize and campaign, and all candidates could use the media freely. The government must be making real progress in creating an independent court system, respecting human rights and basic freedoms as described in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights, allowing independent social economic and political groups to form, guaranteeing free speech and press freedom including letting private media companies operate, and protecting the right to private property. The government cannot include Nicolas Maduro or anyone who has been sanctioned by the US Office of Foreign Assets Control or is wanted by the US Department of Justice. The government must have lifted a contempt order from January 11 2016 against the National Assembly and restored all powers and protections for lawmakers. The government must stop interfering with political parties and candidates including lifting court interventions and restrictions on presidential candidates. The government must release all political prisoners and allow international human rights groups to investigate Venezuelan prisons. The government must dissolve the Colectivos (pro-government groups) and any security services accused of serious human rights violations. The government must genuinely try to send to the US all people wanted by the US Department of Justice for crimes committed in America. The government must not support any group in another country that tries to violently overthrow that country's government. The government must allow independent international human rights monitors to work freely throughout Venezuela. The government must free all hostages and US citizens who are illegally and wrongfully detained.

sections.title: Section 201. United States Policy Regarding Participation of Venezuela in International Financial Institutions
sections.text: The Treasury Secretary must tell US representatives at international financial institutions to use America's voice and vote to oppose letting representatives of Nicolas Maduro or any undemocratic government that follows him be members of these institutions. After the President decides that a democratically elected government is in power in Venezuela, the President should take steps to support seating representatives of the democratic government in these institutions. The Treasury Secretary must then tell US representatives to oppose any assistance from these institutions to Venezuela unless the US Government believes it helps create a stable foundation for democratic government in Venezuela. International financial institutions include the International Monetary Fund, the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development, the International Development Association, the International Finance Corporation, the Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency, and the Inter-American Development Bank.

sections.title: Section 202. United States Policy Regarding Participation of Venezuela in the Organization of American States
sections.text: The President must tell the US representative to the Organization of American States to use America's voice and vote to oppose any measure that would let an undemocratic Venezuelan government participate in the Organization of American States. This continues until the President decides that a democratically elected government is in power in Venezuela.

sections.title: Section 203. Authorization of Support for Democratic and Human Rights Groups and International Observers
sections.text: Despite other laws, the President can provide help and support to individuals and independent nongovernmental organizations to support democracy-building efforts in Venezuela. The President must take steps to encourage the Organization of American States to create a special emergency fund specifically for sending human rights observers and people and organizations that support and observe elections in Venezuela. The President should provide at least five million dollars of US voluntary contributions to the Organization of American States only for this special fund. The President should tell the US representative to encourage other member countries to join in calling for Venezuela to allow immediate deployment of independent human rights monitors throughout Venezuela and on-site visits by the Inter-American Commission on Human Rights. When doing this, the President must make sure no funds or other help goes to an undemocratic Venezuelan government.

sections.title: Section 204. Support for the People of Venezuela
sections.text: The President can provide help through independent nongovernmental organizations to support humanitarian projects in Venezuela that meet basic human needs, build democracy, provide education, support non-commercial development projects, and protect the environment. This is allowed under General License 29 of the Office of Foreign Assets Control of the Treasury Department. The President must create safeguards to make sure any help provided does not give material benefit to the Maduro regime and is used only for its intended purposes and only for the use and benefit of the Venezuelan people.

sections.title: Section 301. Definitions
sections.text: In this part of the law, entity means a partnership, association, trust, joint venture, corporation, group, subgroup, or organization. Government of Venezuela includes the state and government of Venezuela, any political subdivision agency or part of it including the Central Bank of Venezuela and Petroleos de Venezuela SA, any person owned or controlled directly or indirectly by these entities, and any person that has acted or claimed to act directly or indirectly for or on behalf of these entities including as a member of Nicolas Maduro's regime or any undemocratic government that follows. Person means an individual or entity. United States person means a US citizen or legal permanent resident, any entity organized under US laws including foreign branches, and any person physically located in the United States.

sections.title: Section 302. Blocking International Support for a Nondemocratic Government in Venezuela
sections.text: The President should encourage governments of countries that trade with Venezuela to restrict their trade and credit relations with Venezuela in a way that matches the purposes of this law. The President can impose sanctions on any country that provides assistance to Nicolas Maduro or any undemocratic government that follows him. These sanctions can include determining that the country's government is not eligible for non-humanitarian assistance under the Foreign Assistance Act or assistance or sales under the Arms Export Control Act, and determining that the country is not eligible for forgiveness or reduction of debt owed to the US Government. The authority to impose these sanctions ends when the President decides that a democratically elected government is in power in Venezuela. Assistance to Venezuela means help provided by grant, favorable sale, guaranty, insurance, or any other means on terms more favorable than generally available in the market, whether as a loan, lease, credit, or otherwise. This includes subsidies for exports to Venezuela, favorable tariff treatment of Venezuelan articles, and exchange reduction or forgiveness of debt owed by Venezuela in return for an equity interest in Venezuelan property investment or operation. This does not include humanitarian assistance including food donations made available to nongovernmental organizations or individuals in Venezuela, or exports of medicines or medical supplies instruments or equipment.

sections.title: Section 303. Financial Sanctions with Respect to Debt Instruments of Maduro Regime
sections.text: Starting when this law is enacted, all transactions by a US person or within the United States that relate to, provide financing for, or otherwise deal in debt instruments issued by for or on behalf of Petroleos de Venezuela SA or Nicolas Maduro's regime or any undemocratic government that follows are prohibited. This includes entering into any transaction in debt instruments with a maturity of more than ninety days issued by Petroleos de Venezuela SA on or after this law is enacted, debt instruments with a maturity of more than thirty days or equity issued by the Maduro regime on or after this law is enacted, bonds issued by the Maduro regime before this law is enacted, or dividend payments or other profit distributions to the Maduro regime from any entity owned or controlled directly or indirectly by the Maduro regime. It also prohibits the direct or indirect purchase of securities from the Maduro regime except for certain debt instruments, purchasing any debt owed to the Maduro regime including accounts receivable, entering into any transaction related to any debt owed to the Maduro regime that is pledged as collateral after May 21 2018 including accounts receivable, entering into any transaction involving the selling transferring assigning or pledging as collateral by the Maduro regime of any equity interest in any entity in which the Maduro regime has fifty percent or greater ownership interest, and entering into any transaction that evades or avoids has the purpose of evading or avoiding causes a violation of or attempts to violate any of these prohibitions. The Treasury Secretary in consultation with the Secretary of State can take actions including making rules and regulations as necessary to implement this section and can delegate this authority to other officers and agencies of the US Government. All US Government agencies must take all appropriate measures within their authority to carry out this section.

sections.title: Section 304. Sanctions with Respect to Cryptocurrency and Related Technologies in Venezuela
sections.text: Starting when this law is enacted, the following transactions are prohibited: any transaction by a US person or within the United States that relates to provides financing for or otherwise deals in any digital currency digital coin or digital token that was issued by for or on behalf of Nicolas Maduro's regime or any undemocratic government that follows, and any transaction that evades or avoids has the purpose of evading or avoiding causes a violation of or attempts to violate this prohibition. These prohibitions apply to the extent provided by statutes or in regulations orders directives or licenses that may be issued under this section, and despite any contract entered into or any license or permit granted before this law is enacted. The Treasury Secretary in consultation with the Secretary of State can take actions including making rules and regulations as necessary to implement this section and can delegate this authority to other officers and agencies of the US Government. All US Government agencies must take all appropriate measures within their authority to carry out this section.

sections.title: Section 305. Blocking Property of the Government of Venezuela
sections.text: The President must use all powers granted by the International Emergency Economic Powers Act as necessary to block and prohibit all transactions in all property and interests in property of the Government of Venezuela and certain other people if such property and interests in property are in the United States come within the United States or are or come within the possession or control of a US person. Other people covered include anyone determined by the Treasury Secretary in consultation with the Secretary of State to have materially assisted sponsored or provided financial material or technological support for or goods or services to or in support of any person whose property and interests in property are blocked, or to be owned or controlled by or to have acted or claimed to act for or on behalf of directly or indirectly any person whose property and interests in property are blocked. Any transaction that evades or avoids has the purpose of evading or avoiding causes a violation of or attempts to violate the prohibition is also prohibited. These prohibitions apply to the extent provided by statutes or in regulations orders directives or licenses that may be issued under this section and despite any contract entered into or any license or permit granted before this law is enacted. The Treasury Secretary in consultation with the Secretary of State can take actions including making rules and regulations as necessary to implement this section and can delegate this authority to other officers and agencies of the US Government. All US Government agencies must take all appropriate measures within their authority to carry out this section.

sections.title: Section 306. Termination of Sanctions
sections.text: When the President tells Congress that a democratically elected government is in power in Venezuela, the President must take steps to end the sanctions imposed under this part of the law. After taking action to end these sanctions, the President must immediately notify Congress of that action and submit to Congress at least every one hundred eighty days for three years a report on the progress being made by Venezuela toward establishing a democratically elected government. Congress can review this termination through a joint resolution of disapproval which is a joint resolution that says Congress disapproves the President's action to terminate the sanctions. An action taken by the President to end sanctions will stop being effective if Congress enacts a joint resolution of disapproval about that action. A joint resolution of disapproval introduced in the Senate goes to the Committee on Foreign Relations and in the House goes to the Committee on Foreign Affairs. There are specific procedures for considering these resolutions quickly in both chambers. No more than one joint resolution of disapproval can be considered in each chamber during the first one hundred eighty day period after the President notifies Congress and during each one hundred eighty day period after that. This paragraph is enacted as an exercise of Congress's rulemaking power and can be changed by either chamber like any other rule.

sections.title: Section 307. Implementation; Penalties
sections.text: The President can use all authorities provided under sections 203 and 205 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to carry out this part of the law. A person that violates attempts to violate conspires to violate or causes a violation of this part of the law or any regulation license or order issued to carry out this part of the law will be subject to the penalties set forth in the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to the same extent as a person that commits an unlawful act under that law. The President can waive the application of sanctions under this part of the law for a foreign person if the President not later than ten days before the waiver takes effect determines and tells Congress that such a waiver is in the vital national security interest of the United States. The President must submit with this certification a detailed justification explaining the reasons for the waiver.

sections.title: Section 308. Report on Specific Licenses that Authorize Transactions with Sanctioned Persons
sections.text: No later than one hundred eighty days after this law is enacted and every one hundred eighty days after that until ten years after this law is enacted, the Treasury Secretary working with the Secretary of State must submit to Congress a report that includes a list of specific licenses issued by the Treasury Secretary during the previous one hundred eighty days that authorize any transaction with a person who has been sanctioned under sections 303 304 or 305, and an estimate of funds the Maduro regime has accessed as a result of licenses issued by the Office of Foreign Assets Control since January 20 2021 for the first report or during the previous one hundred eighty days for later reports. The report must include for each entity that received a specific license the name of the entity a brief description of the activity authorized by the license and a brief justification of why the Office of Foreign Assets Control granted the license. The Treasury Secretary must quickly provide to Congress a copy of any license identified in a report if a Member of Congress requests a copy within sixty days after the report is submitted. The Treasury Secretary must identify any proprietary information submitted by any private sector representative and mark such information as business confidential information in each report and any submission. Business confidential information is considered a trade secret and commercial or financial information that is exempt from disclosure under the Freedom of Information Act without the express approval of the private party. Each report and any submission must be submitted in unclassified form but may contain a classified annex. This section has no force or effect after the end of the ten year period beginning when this law is enacted.

sections.title: Section 309. Report on Foreign Persons Doing Business with the Maduro Regime
sections.text: No later than one hundred eighty days after this law is enacted, the Secretary of State working with the heads of other relevant Federal agencies must submit to Congress a report that identifies any foreign person that engages in or has engaged in a significant transaction or transactions or any other dealings with or has provided material support to or for the Government of Venezuela, any person the President determines to be knowingly responsible for or complicit in or responsible for ordering controlling or otherwise directing or to have participated in directly or indirectly actions or policies that significantly undermine democratic processes or institutions in Venezuela significant acts of violence or conduct that constitute serious human rights abuse including against persons involved in antigovernment protests in Venezuela on or after February 1 2014 actions that prohibit limit or penalize the exercise of freedom of expression or peaceful assembly in Venezuela or significant public corruption by senior officials within the Government of Venezuela, or any entity that has or whose members have engaged in any such activity. The report must also identify foreign persons that operate in the mining financial energy shipping or shipbuilding sector of Venezuela's economy, operate in the ports free trade zones or special economic zones of Venezuela, are owned or controlled by a foreign person described above, or have knowingly materially assisted sponsored or provided significant financial material or technological support for or goods or services in support of a foreign person described above.

sections.title: Section 401. Assistance for the People of Venezuela
sections.text: The President must develop a plan for providing assistance to Venezuela under a democratically elected government including a strategy for distributing assistance under the plan. Assistance to Venezuela under a democratically elected government provided under the plan may include food medicine medical supplies and equipment and assistance to meet emergency energy needs as is necessary to meet the basic human needs of the Venezuelan people, assistance under development assistance and economic support fund programs, assistance under the Food for Peace Act, financing guarantees and other forms of assistance provided by the Export-Import Bank of the United States, assistance provided by the Trade and Development Agency, and Peace Corps programs. Assistance under the plan will be provided through relevant US Federal departments and agencies and nongovernmental organizations and private and voluntary organizations whether within or outside the United States including humanitarian educational labor and private sector organizations. The President must take the necessary steps to communicate to the Venezuelan people the plan for assistance developed. No later than ninety days after this law is enacted, the President must submit to Congress a report describing in detail the plan developed. When the President tells Congress that a democratically elected government is in power in Venezuela, the President must commence the delivery and distribution of assistance to the democratically elected government under the plan developed. No later than sixty days after the end of each fiscal year, the President must submit to Congress a report on the assistance provided under the plan including a description of each type of assistance and the amounts expended for such assistance during the preceding fiscal year and a description of the assistance to be provided under the plan in the fiscal year in which the report is submitted. The Secretary of State must designate a coordinating official who will be responsible for implementing the strategy for distributing assistance, ensuring the speedy and efficient distribution of such assistance, and ensuring coordination among and appropriate oversight by the agencies of the United States that provide assistance including resolving any disputes among such agencies. Any changes in the assistance to be provided under the plan may not be made unless the Secretary of State notifies Congress at least fifteen days in advance according to the procedures applicable to reprogramming notifications under the Foreign Assistance Act. Assistance may be provided under this section only if amounts are authorized to be appropriated and are appropriated to provide such assistance. The President must take the necessary steps to seek to obtain the agreement of other countries and of international financial institutions and multilateral organizations to provide to a democratically elected government in Venezuela assistance comparable to that provided by the United States under this section and to work with such countries institutions and organizations to coordinate all such assistance programs.

sections.title: Section 402. Report on Trade and Investment Relations Between the United States and Venezuela
sections.text: When the President tells Congress that a democratically elected government is in power in Venezuela, the President must submit to the House Committee on Ways and Means the Senate Committee on Finance and the appropriate congressional committees a report that describes acts policies and practices that constitute significant barriers to or distortions of United States trade in goods or services or foreign direct investment with respect to Venezuela, and policy objectives of the United States regarding trade relations with a democratically elected government in Venezuela and the reasons for such objectives including possible reciprocal extension of nondiscriminatory trade treatment (most-favored-nation treatment). Regarding the policy objectives, the President must consult with the House Committee on Ways and Means the Senate Committee on Finance and the appropriate congressional committees, and seek advice from the appropriate advisory committees established under the Trade Act of 1974.

sections.title: Section 501. Effect on Lawful United States Government Activities
sections.text: Nothing in this law prohibits any lawfully authorized investigative protective or intelligence activity of a law enforcement agency or of an intelligence agency of the United States.

sections.title: Section 502. Exception Relating to Importation of Goods
sections.text: Despite any other provision of this law, the authorities and requirements to impose sanctions under this law do not include the authority or a requirement to impose sanctions on the importation of goods. In this section, good means any article natural or manmade substance material supply or manufactured product including inspection and test equipment and excluding technical data.
